content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance Administration Executive/Legal Enforcement Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-4441 322-4444

February 28, 1983


Ronald L. Schneider
Principal Deputy County Counsel
County of Los Angeles
Office of the County Counsel
648 Hall of Administration
Los Angeles, CA 90012

Re: Your Letter to Robert M. Stern
regarding AB 1040 as applied
to the Los Angeles County
Transportation Commission, Our
File No. A-83-047

Dear Mr. Schneider:

I have been asked by our General Counsel, Robert M. Stern,
to respond to your letter to him regarding AB 1040 as applied to
the Los Angeles County Transportation Commission ("LATC"). The
LATC is involved in transportation planning and coordination for
Los Angeles County. Among other activities, it acts as a funnel
for consideration of transportation plans and transportation
grant applications, which must be approved by it prior to being
submitted to other agencies. (See, Public Utilities Code
Section 130301, et seq.) Some of the transit operators subject
to its jurisdiction are private entities, others are public
agencies. You have asked our advice regarding the applicability
of newly enacted Government Code Section 84308 (AB 1040) to the
members of LATC.

First, as you have recognized, the blanket exemption
contained in Section 84308(d) for legislative bodies does not
apply to LATC. While its members are elected officials, they
are not directly elected to that body as set forth in Regulation
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.1. Thus, LATC is covered by
Section 84308 generally. However, the extent to which Section
84308 is applicable may be limited by the nature of the
decisions which LATC makes. Since many of the applicants are
public entities, which do not make campaign contributions, the
situations in which disqualification would be required are
reduced. However, if a private party who has contributed to a

Ronald L. Schneider
February 28, 1983
Page Two


LATC Commissioner has a financial interest1/ in the decision,
and actively supports or opposes2/ the application,
disqualification may be required (if other requirements are
present3/). In addition, a situation where a party who is the
applicant has made a contribution could require
disqualification.

To summarize, LATC is not subject to any blanket exemption
from the operation of Government Code Section 84308.
Consequently, its members will have to carefully evaluate
participation in LATC decisions where private parties are
applicants, or actively support or oppose a matter, to determine
whether any basis for disqualification exists.

I enclose a copy of the Commission's recently adopted
regulations interpreting Section 84308. When we have completed
development of a model disclosure form for use, when needed, we
will send it to you. If you have further questions, please do
not hesitate to contact me. I may be reached at 916/322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel
Legal Division

REL:km


1/ See, Government Code Section 87103.

2/ See, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.4.

3/ The contribution would have to exceed $250 during the
preceding 12 month period and not be returned within the 30-day
period set forth in Government Code Section 84308(b).

November 13, 1982                                226-9119

Los Angeles County Transportation Commission
354 South Spring Street, Suite 500
Los Angeles, California 90013

Attention  Rick Richmond, Executive Director

Re:  Application of AB 1040 to Los Angeles
     County Transportation Commission

Gentlemen:

     In response to your request and that of several members
of the Commission, I have reviewed the provisions of AB 1040
with respect to its applicability to the Los Angeles County
Transportation Commission.  I have also reviewed the regulations
of the Fair Political Practices Commission (FPPC), the State
agency responsible for the administration and implementation of
California's political reform statutes, which clarify and
interpret the provisions of AB 1040.

     I have concluded that AB 1040 is not applicable to
the members of the Commission.

     AB 1040, which will become operative January 1, 1983,
prohibits a member of a quasi-judicial board or commission
from accepting, soliciting or directing a campaign contribution
of $250.00 or more within 12 months before or three months after
a decision.  Each application or active supporter of
an application for a license, permit or other entitlement for
use pending before the board or commission.

     The statute applies to members of legislative bodies
such as boards of supervisors and councils while acting
as a body or committee.  It does not apply to members on
members on another body or commission which
"considers individual matters or licenses, permits, or
entitlements for use." (Gov. Code, added by AB 1040.)

     Since all five members of the Los Angeles County Board
of Supervisors and members of councils are members
of the Commission, they are not prohibited from
them if the Commission is such a body under AB 1040.

AB 1040 does not define either "quasi-judicial board
or commission" or "licenses, permits or entitlements for use".
However, the regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission clarify both AB 1040 no.

Section 14430.1(c) (J Cal. Admin. Code), one of FPPC's
AB 1040 regulations, provides that a "'quasi-judicial board or
commission' means any appointed or elected governmental body
other than a legislative body, which may make decisions concerning
licenses, permits, or other entitlements for use...".

Section 14430.2, subsections (a) and (b), (2 Cal. Admin.
Code) anchor FPPC regulation, specifics

"(a) An 'application for a license, permit or other
entitlement for use' includes, but is not limited to, any
proceeding to grant, deny, revoke, restrict, or modify:

(1) Any professional, business educational, or
training license;

(2) Any land use permit, license or other action
relating to zoning, variances, subdivision maps,
building permits, leases of public land or facilities,
or other similar proceeding;

(3) Any permit or license to carry on any business
activity;

(4) Any other entitlement for use, including
allocation of data for horseshoeing, rate-setting for
public utilities, and other allotments, excepting
waivers affecting the operations of any private business
entity.

(b) The awarding of a contract or other action taken
for the purpose of entering into a contract for the provision of
goods or services to, or employment by, an agency, is not an
'application for a license, permit or other entitlement for use'".

The authority and duty of the Los Angeles County
Transportation Commission is contained in the County Transportation Commissions Act (Division 12, commencing with Section 130001,
of the Government Code). The Commission essentially coordinates
public transit services, allocating highway and mass transit funds,
and is responsible for the construction and operation of a public
mass transit system, in Los Angeles County.

- J -

It is clear that the Commission's statutory activities
permit or other consideration or processing of "licenses,
in AB 1040 and the regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission." It follows then that the Commission is not a
"quasi-judicial" board or commission" regulated under AB 1040.

Under these circumstances, AB 1040 is not applicable to
the members of the Los Angeles County Transportation Commission.

If you have any further questions concerning AB 1040,
I will be pleased to discuss them with you or the Commission.

Very truly yours,

JOHN H. LARSON
County Counsel

By
Ronald L. Schneider
Principal Deputy County Counsel

RLS:tr/22A

cc: State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
Attention: Mr. Robert M. Storm, General Counsel

COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES
OFFICE OF THE COUNTY COUNSEL
640 HALL OF ADMINISTRATION
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90012

JOHN H LARSON, COUNTY COUNSEL

November 16, 1982                                    226-8119


Mr. Robert M. Stern, General Counsel
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street Building
P.O. Box 307
Sacramento, California  95804

     Re: Application of AB 1040 to Los Angeles
          County Transportation Commission

Dear Mr. Stern:

     Thank you for a copy of the regulations of the
Commission relating to the interpretation of AB 1040.  I am
enclosing for your information, as you suggested, a copy of
my advice letter to the Los Angeles County Transportation
Commission wherein I concluded that AB 1040 is not applicable
to members of the Commission.

     If you have any questions concerning this matter, or
if you differ with my conclusions, I would appreciate your
advising me as soon as possible.

                                   Very truly yours,

                                   JOHN H. LARSON
                                   County Counsel

                                   [signature]
                              By
                                   Donald L. Schneider
                                   Principal Deputy County Counsel

RLS:tr/22A

Encl.
